TextENGNew: ...For I am the guardian deity of this village.
changeReason: New translation uses different proper nouns or naming, which could affect understanding of who/what is being referenced.

TextENGNew: At our meeting place, just as expected, Rena and Mion had arrived first and were waiting for me.
"Ahahaha! Hurry up, Keiichi-kun, hurry~!"
"Honestly, Kei-chan, you're so slow!"
"Okay, counting down from five~, three, two, one..."
"Wh-wh-whooooaaa~~!!"
I threw my weight into the pedals and accelerated all at once. Like a gust of wind I shot between the two of them—then turned the handlebars right into an inertial driftttt!!
CRASH BANG CLATTER~~!!
"K-Keiichi-kun, are you all right?!"
"U-ugh... yeah, I suppose trying a reverse drift on a bicycle was never going to work... ow, ow!"
"Oh, honestly, what are you doing... here, grab on!"
I took Mion's outstretched hand and got to my feet, brushing the dirt from my back and sides. ...I stung here and there, but strangely enough I didn't have a single real injury.
"On the other hand, the bicycle is deceased. ...What are you going to do about that?"
She said it with an exasperated look as she heaved my bicycle upright.
changeReason: Old says 'stunned look' while JPN 呆れた顔 means 'exasperated/fed-up expression'; different emotional characterization.

TextENGNew: ...My beloved bicycle had not fared so well—the chain was snapped, the front brake was barely functional, the basket was broken, a spoke had come out... what one might call a total wreck.
``I mean~, Kei-chan, you just do way too many reckless things.@k@vS14/03/220300300. You'd barely healed the fastest of anyone in the club, and then you had to go pick a fight with the principal~!``
changeReason: Both say Mion lifted the bike; 'stunned' vs 'exasperated' are slightly different but core action is same.

TextENGNew: "The one who came up with that punishment game was you, Mion!"
"Ahahaha! But the principal was actually impressed! He said 'You're the second person since the war to come this close to grazing my clothes'—right? That's our club president for you~?"

TextENGNew: ...The price I paid for that distinction had been extraordinary, though.
"That's right! From this day forth, you may all address me as the Conqueror of the Century's End!!"

TextENGNew: Shion Sonozaki and Rena Ryuugu. The most catastrophically powerful—rather than simply the strongest—tag team had come together, and I had failed to stop it. How greatly would this shape the rest of my life...? That was not something I wanted to think about. I refused to think about it.
changeReason: New translation significantly restructures the content, potentially altering the reader's understanding.

TextENGNew: "Come on! Let's get moving, Rena! Kei-chan, get yourself ready!"
``Ah, I want to, nothing would make me happier, but...@k@vS14/01/220100406. with the bicycle in this state...``
"Oh, right... well, can't be helped. Then you can ride double with me—"
"Haww~, riding double?! Ooh ooh, I want that~! Keiichi-kun driving, Mii-chan in the back~! Chest pressed against back, bumpety bump☆ haww haww~♪"
changeReason: New translation significantly restructures the content, potentially altering the reader's understanding.

TextENGNew: "~~~~! O-okay, I'm going ahead! Kei-chan can run, see ya!!"
"Ahhh?! Mii-chan, wait~~!!"
"Hey! I'm on foot?! You expect me to keep up with that speed?! HEYYY!!"
—That was a future I had dreamed of, for so long, so long.
Every day bright and full of noise.
Happy hours when I couldn't sleep for the excitement of the day yet to come.
"...Oh, they're here, they're here! Over here, over here~!"
As though they had been waiting impatiently, Shion, Satoko, and Rika-chan stood at the entrance to the shrine.
"Yaho~, Shii-chan♪ It's such nice weather today~!"
"...I didn't think we invited Shion to today's gathering~?"
"What are you talking about. Wherever Satoko goes, what would she do without her big sister here to look after her?"
"I didn't want to invite her either, but... Rika let it slip."
"...Satoko's lunch starting tomorrow will be carrot glacé, and stuffed peppers with meat, and—"
"N-nee-nee being here makes this sort of gathering so much more, so much more f-fun and wonderful! O-hohohoh!!"

...She's been thoroughly trained, Satoko has. Watching her there—smiling on the outside while her heart sang something like a tearful ballad—Rika-chan patted her on the head and offered a consoling smile.
changeReason: New translation significantly restructures the content, potentially altering the reader's understanding.

TextENGNew: "As long as you're alive, good things are sure to come. Faiit ohh, is what I say."
"...If you believe that, could you at least stop saying it with that smug grin on your face?!"
And several minutes and seconds behind Mion and Rena—
—drenched in sweat and gasping for breath, I finally arrived at the shrine.
"...As usual, Keiichi-san, you have a truly carefree relationship with punctuality."
"His learning capability is below that of a word processor—niipa~♪"
"...Why did you run here?"
"Lower body training? Hm? Right, sis?"
"There's-absolute-ly-noth-ing-to-it!!"
"...Why are you angry?"
"...A-anyway, something to drink... oh Shion, let me have that barley tea—"
"Oh, that's... udon broth."
SPIT-TAKE!!
The same everyday moments, repeating.
But wherever you looked, whatever you were doing, there was laughter.
Days so dense and full that time seemed to race by too quickly—wonderful.
"...And so—as a celebration of Rika-chan's full recovery yesterday, marking the return of all club members to full health, we are delighted to present today's humble celebratory lunch gathering!"
"YAAAAHHHHH!!"
A tremendous wave of applause broke out all at once, and the cheers of the club members—which was to say, the entire class—rang out together.
And everyone poured juice and soda into paper cups—alcohol was strictly forbidden for minors, obviously—dropped in ice, and got ready for the toast.
...A week had passed since then. Today was the first Sunday of July.

The day after the incident, when we had shown up to school covered in injuries, our classmates Daiki and Toshiro and the others, as well as Chie-sensei, had understandably been alarmed and had rushed to ask what had happened.
changeReason: New translation significantly restructures the content, potentially altering the reader's understanding.

TextENGNew: We couldn't exactly explain the real circumstances, so we laughed it off and said we had injured ourselves researching a certain club activity, then went back to our normal lives.

Even so, Rika-chan had been visibly subdued, and I had been at a loss for how to cheer her up. But the very next day she came back to club activities in good spirits, and—remarkably—actually won, astonishing everyone.
On the way home that day... when I'd asked how she was doing, Rika-chan smiled and said—
"—Hanyuu's greatest wish was for me to spend my days happily."
"So I've decided to keep on smiling, with everything I have."
Looking at her then, I thought from the bottom of my heart—though it was perhaps rude to say—that she had grown up.
Just as Hanyuu had wanted her to...
...Strangely, no one except me, Rika-chan, and Takano-san had witnessed the moment when Hanyuu shielded Takano-san and then vanished.
According to Mion and the others, when they came to themselves, Keiichi and Rika-chan were already in tears right beside where Takano-san was being taken away.
changeReason: New translation uses different proper nouns or naming, which could affect understanding of who/what is being referenced.

TextENGNew: So Hanyuu... was something that only Rika-chan and I would carry with us, always—that was the promise we had made between ourselves.
"And now, let me ask our beloved class teacher Chie-sensei to lead the toast as our representative! Please!"
"Long live curry!!"
"—Cheers!!"
"Why is it curry here too?!"
And we all spread out our bento boxes and dug in together, clattering chopsticks and exclaiming over each other's food.

As expected, the most popular item was Rena Special Mark II (named by the creator herself)—the younger students who had clearly been eyeing it converged on it all at once, and Rena's signature "take you home♪" was deployed freely upon the thoroughly charmed.
...There were also requests from the girls to be taught how to cook, which seemed to make her genuinely happy, so all was well.
...I'd better check her bag on the way home for any stowaways.
By the way, there had been a proposal to make this a barbecue instead, but it was rejected by Mion's fierce opposition.
...Well, given that any excuse involving an open fire would bring the adults gathering, and this time we'd probably end up being asked about wedding dates and honeymoon destinations.
And so—rather than democracy by majority vote, the committee chair exercised her absolute authority and amended the proposal to a bento potluck.
...That was followed by Shion's agitation of 'the chairperson is playing favorites for Sonozaki~!' which caused a considerable uproar in the classroom and was quite a headache to manage.
Oh, yes—speaking of Shion...
"—Shion. I heard from Mion—you're going to apply to nursing school?"
"Yes, next year. ...It's good timing, I think. I'll give it a real try."
Shion said it with a grin, raising her paper cup.
...I could easily guess that it was for Satoshi's sake.
"Well, that's certainly part of it. But not only that."
"I've started to feel that healing someone and helping them recover is so much more enjoyable and rewarding than hurting someone. Something like that."
"Heh heh heh! Satoko, you'd better take care of yourself~? If you don't get healthy fast enough, one day your assigned nurse might turn out to be Shion~?"
Hearing that, Satoko's expression went into the full Munch's Scream, contorting dramatically.
"Fuwaaah!! That would be a nightmare! Truly a living hell~~!!"
"Excuse me, Satoko! What exactly do you mean by that—hmm?!"
"Come on, I'm mostly joking."
"You mean mostly *serious*!!"
"Ahaha! To begin with, Shion as a nurse is just impossible! Being a nurse means difficult exams, tough practical training, and then an enormously responsible job day and night without rest—"
"I'll be fine. I'm a better student than you, at least, sis."
"Oh? Then quiz time. Simple question—what is the capital of the United States?!"
"New York."
"Wha?! That's... correct...!!"
No, it isn't. It's Washington. ...What kind of spectacularly low-level competition is this? Do either of them actually intend to take entrance exams?!
Surrounded by so many friends.
Helping each other, being helped by each other,@rmoving forward—slowly, little by little, but surely.
changeReason: New translation uses different proper nouns or naming, which could affect understanding of who/what is being referenced.

TextENGNew: Wishing for another's happiness.@rAnd hoping they would wish the same for me.
...And then—this was very happy news.
Since the incident, Satoko's symptoms had apparently been improving at a remarkable rate.
Unfortunately, the Hinamizawa Syndrome research project was going to be closed as scheduled in three years—
—but thanks to the knowledge and samples gained from Satoko's treatment, a meaningful lead toward a fundamental cure had apparently been found, even if not a perfect one.
If everything continued to go well, the endemic disease might be completely eradicated before the project closed—the supervisor had said, with evident happiness.
Each of us,@rfinding our own path@rand beginning to walk it.
And ahead on the way forward,@rsmall but strong,@ra certain radiance.
changeReason: Both say 'ahead, small but strong, a certain brightness/radiance' - minor rephrasing.

TextENGNew:
—Yes.
There was hope.
To cool myself down a little after the heat of the gathering, I stepped away from the group and sat down in the shade of a tree.
Truly wonderful friends.
Truly joyful days.
All of it—
—was an irreplaceable, precious treasure that we had claimed for ourselves.
Hanyuu.
Can you see it?
Everyone's smiling faces.
Hanyuu.
Can you hear it?
Everyone's happy voices.
You are no longer beside me.
When I think of that, I am still—lonely, and sad.
changeReason: New translation uses different proper nouns or naming, which could affect understanding of who/what is being referenced.

TextENGNew: But, Hanyuu.
This world—it is a world I could only obtain because of you.
And it is a world we were all able to protect together, because you helped us understand.
So as long as I live in this world—
—I will never forget you.
And you will not have truly disappeared from this world.
That's right, isn't it—Hanyuu?
Because you were always together with me.
And surely, from now on too—
Of course, Rika...
changeReason: Original translation was missing entirely; new translation provides: Of course, Rika...

TextENGNew: Just then.
In the moment the wind passed by.
I thought I heard—a voice that sounded like Hanyuu.
...Hanyuu.
Now that I can no longer see your face, I am so very lonely.
Now that I can no longer hear your voice, I am so very sad.
But I have made up my mind.
The happiness you wished for me.
The joy you hoped I would find.
I want to wish the same for so many others.
I want to pray, always and forever, for the happiness and joy of the people I hold dear.
That is the duty of one born into the Furude family—no.
It is the way of life that Furude Rika, loved by you, can be your greatest pride—
changeReason: New translation conveys noticeably different meaning or nuance from the original.

when the higurashi cry—
